objective: Close F-0077 by disabling stale or non-succeeded knowledge recommendation review actions in the admin UI and independently revalidating the current durable server review command: task/candidate identity, question revision and available status, candidate pending status, active/recommendable same-scope knowledge_node eligibility, transaction locks, atomic binding, and stable conflict response. Do not change schema, recommendation generation/failure semantics, authorization, or Provider behavior.
blockedFiles: schema, migration or persistent database execution
recommendation generation, runner, failure recovery or Provider behavior
new role, entitlement, authorization scope, quota or default capability
external service, secret/env, deployment, PR or force-push

authoritativeChainEvidence.uiApiCallers: The recommendation panel already computes isStale and displays the expired message, but review buttons only check readability and pending candidate state. The existing review handler sends taskPublicId, expectedQuestionUpdatedAt and candidatePublicIds to the dedicated command.
authoritativeChainEvidence.routeService: The dedicated POST command parses confirm/ignore identity and revision inputs, maps null/superseded repository results to stable 409621, and audits failed review attempts.
authoritativeChainEvidence.repository: The current durable repository locks question, task and candidates; requires succeeded task, exact question revision, available question, pending selected candidates and active recommendable profession/level-compatible knowledge nodes before atomically replacing bindings and recording review status.
authoritativeChainEvidence.schema: Durable kn_recommendation_task and kn_recommendation_candidate state already exists from the current master. No schema or migration source is needed.
authoritativeChainEvidence.tests: UI RED will prove stale candidates remain actionable before the fix; route/repository-focused static regressions revalidate the dedicated command and F-0053 binding eligibility without changing server semantics.
authoritativeChainEvidence.requirements: RAG knowledge requirements require human confirmation and superseding old question revisions; disabled nodes are not recommendable and moved nodes use current scope.
authoritativeChainEvidence.nonClaims: No real PostgreSQL execution, recommendation failure recovery change, Provider call, schema, permission, dependency or mechanism change.
